objective: Add local-first coverage inventory and enforcement for governance GitHub Actions workflows so deterministic runner failures are caught before push and GitHub-only jobs have explicit exemptions.
scope_boundary: Classify every `.github/workflows/*.yml` file by local coverage strategy.
Add deterministic enforcement that fails when workflow coverage inventory drifts.
Prioritize `governance-check.yml`, `check-pr-commit-scope.yml`, and reusable deterministic governance workflows.
Keep CI authoritative; local-first coverage is an upstream filter and contract guard, not a full Actions replay.
Verify with real local command runs and GitHub Actions evidence before closeout.
out_of_scope: Replaying all GitHub Actions workflows with `act`.
Running scheduled, token-writing, or side-effecting jobs locally as normal pre-push checks.
Changing consumer repository workflows or branch protection.
Changing GitHub rulesets unless the implementation proves local coverage needs a required-status update.
Touching dirty sibling checkouts outside this isolated worktree.
research_summary: After Local CI Gate hardgate wiring (#277/#282), adjacent governance workflows still need a local-first coverage map. The repo has deterministic workflow validators, reusable gates, and scheduled/side-effecting jobs mixed together; the next control is an inventory plus validator that prevents silent drift and documents exemptions.

sprints.name: Plan And Scope
sprints.goal: Land issue-backed planning, cross-review, and execution-scope artifacts before implementation edits.
tasks: Create the GitHub epic with PDCAR acceptance criteria.
Add structured plan, PDCAR, cross-review, execution scope, and same-family implementation exception artifacts.
Mirror the active issue in `OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md` and `docs/PROGRESS.md`.
acceptance_criteria: The execution scope allows only issue #284 implementation paths.
Subagent review findings are represented in the plan evidence.

sprints.name: Inventory And Enforcement
sprints.goal: Add a tracked workflow coverage inventory and a deterministic validator for drift.
tasks: Create a structured inventory covering every workflow file.
Implement a validator that compares the inventory to `.github/workflows/*.yml`.
Require deterministic workflows to declare local, contract, or script/dry-run coverage.
Require GitHub-only workflows to declare explicit exemption rationale.
Add unit tests for missing inventory entries, missing workflow files, missing coverage commands, and accepted exemptions.
acceptance_criteria: The validator passes for the repository inventory.
A new workflow without inventory coverage fails tests.
A deterministic workflow without a coverage command fails tests.
Side-effecting workflows are documented with explicit rationale instead of replayed.

sprints.name: E2E Verification
sprints.goal: Prove the new enforcement locally and in GitHub Actions with real runs.
tasks: Run local unit and contract tests.
Run the Local CI Gate profile against the implementation diff.
Create a PR and let GitHub Actions run the new coverage checks.
If a check fails, fix forward and rerun until green.
Record issue, branch, commit, run IDs, and check names in closeout evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Local tests include a negative-control unit test for missing workflow inventory.
Local CI Gate runs the workflow coverage check.
GitHub Actions passes the updated contract workflow and Local CI Gate.
Closeout evidence cites real command output and Actions run identifiers.

specialist_reviews.focus: Existing local gate profile, execution-scope contract, and likely extension points.
specialist_reviews.summary: Use the existing profile and direct Python test entrypoints; treat generated reports as local-only. One finding about missing local-ci-gate workflow was stale against current origin/main after #277.

specialist_reviews.focus: Workflow classification and high-risk local-first coverage candidates.
specialist_reviews.summary: Recommended split coverage for mixed workflows, with `governance-check.yml`, `check-pr-commit-scope.yml`, and `require-cross-review.yml` as highest-risk. The review counted 20 workflows, while the current branch has 22; the implementation therefore treats filesystem inventory as authoritative.

alternate_model_review.summary: Subagent review is required by session instruction; tooling and workflow classification reviews accepted the inventory/validator approach with caveats that the implementation resolves through filesystem-backed completeness checks.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #284 may add workflow coverage inventory, validator/tests, local profile wiring, contract workflow wiring, planning/status artifacts, and closeout evidence inside hldpro-governance only.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Implement the workflow local coverage inventory and validator, then run local and GitHub Actions E2E verification.

material_deviation_rules: If a workflow requires secrets, schedule context, or write side effects, classify it as script/dry-run or GitHub-only rather than adding a local replay.
If inventory enforcement would require changing consumer repos, stop and create a separate issue.
If Local CI Gate cannot run because sibling roots are dirty, record the blocker and rely on focused tests plus GitHub Actions evidence.
If GitHub Actions fails, fix forward on the same branch and rerun before closeout.
approved_by: operator instruction: PDCAR, follow repo rules, loop e2e, no HITL
